_comment: Tiles agent config. Generates FOCUSED isometric terrain tile sets via PixelLab create-tiles-pro. Every tile: 64px, 28-degree view angle, 50% thickness (depth_ratio 0.5), isometric. Each category is one create-tiles-pro call (~16 tile variations, ~20 generations) and stays on-theme (snow=snow, town=town) — no mixed do-everything tiles. Output downloaded to tiles/<category>/. A separate Maps agent assembles these into worlds. See tiles/README.md.
style_base: cohesive readable pixel art, clean isometric terrain tiles, Grave Seasons / Stardew Valley palette

budget._comment: Shared PixelLab pool (coordination/PROTOCOL.md). Each category ~20 generations.

_categories: Focused tile sets covering the whole world. `id` = folder under tiles/. `description` numbers on-theme variations only.

categories.description: isometric grass terrain tiles, all grass only: 1) lush green grass 2) grass with small yellow flowers 3) grass with white daisies 4) tall grass tufts 5) dry yellow grass 6) mossy grass
isometric dirt terrain tiles, all dirt/soil only: 1) packed brown dirt 2) dry cracked dirt 3) dirt with small pebbles 4) muddy dirt 5) tilled soil 6) dark rich soil
isometric sand terrain tiles, all sand only: 1) pale desert sand 2) rippled sand dunes 3) sand with small rocks 4) wet beach sand 5) golden sand 6) coarse sandy gravel
isometric snow terrain tiles, all snow only: 1) fresh white snow 2) packed snow 3) snow with footprints 4) icy crusted snow 5) snow drift 6) snow over small rocks
isometric ice terrain tiles, all ice only: 1) smooth blue ice 2) cracked ice 3) frozen lake ice 4) frosty ice 5) glacial ice 6) icy slush
isometric water terrain tiles, all water only: 1) calm blue water 2) rippling water 3) deep ocean water 4) shallow clear water 5) sparkling water 6) dark water
isometric stone terrain tiles, all natural stone only: 1) grey rock 2) mossy stone 3) cracked stone 4) smooth boulder stone 5) dark slate 6) pebbled stone
isometric town cobblestone tiles, all paving only: 1) grey cobblestone 2) round cobble path 3) worn cobblestone 4) mossy cobblestone 5) neat cobble pavement 6) dark cobblestone
isometric brick road tiles, all brick paving only: 1) red brick road 2) worn brick 3) herringbone brick 4) grey brick pavement 5) mossy brick 6) cracked brick

isometric castle stone floor tiles, all castle interior stone only: 1) grey castle flagstone 2) polished marble floor 3) stone brick floor 4) cracked dungeon stone 5) royal red carpet on stone 6) dark stone tile
isometric wooden floor tiles, all wood planks only: 1) warm wood planks 2) worn wood floor 3) dark oak planks 4) light pine planks 5) wide plank floor 6) creaky old wood

isometric forest floor tiles, all forest ground only: 1) leafy forest floor 2) mossy ground with ferns 3) pine needle ground 4) forest dirt with roots 5) grass with fallen leaves 6) dark woodland floor
isometric farmland tiles, all farm ground only: 1) tilled soil rows 2) watered soil 3) soil with sprouts 4) hay covered ground 5) dry field 6) furrowed farmland
isometric mountain rock tiles, all rocky mountain only: 1) grey mountain rock 2) craggy stone 3) snow-capped rock 4) layered rock strata 5) dark basalt 6) rocky scree
isometric swamp terrain tiles, all swamp/marsh only: 1) murky swamp water 2) muddy marsh 3) mossy bog 4) reedy wetland 5) algae water 6) soggy peat
isometric volcanic terrain tiles, all volcanic only: 1) glowing lava 2) cooled black basalt 3) cracked magma crust 4) obsidian rock 5) ashen ground 6) ember-flecked rock
isometric cave floor tiles, all cave interior only: 1) damp grey cave floor 2) rocky cave ground 3) cave floor with pebbles 4) crystal-flecked stone 5) dark cavern floor 6) wet cave rock
isometric desert terrain tiles, all arid desert only: 1) cracked desert clay 2) rocky desert ground 3) reddish sand 4) dry hardpan 5) desert gravel 6) sun-baked earth

isometric grassy cliff and hillside edge tiles, tall vertical rock face topped with grass, for raised terrain edges: 1) grass cliff front face 2) grass cliff left corner 3) grass cliff right corner 4) grass slope 5) grassy ledge 6) rocky grass drop

isometric grey stone cliff and rock wall tiles, tall vertical rock face for plateaus and canyons: 1) stone cliff front face 2) stone cliff corner 3) layered rock strata face 4) cracked cliff 5) mossy cliff 6) tall boulder wall
isometric snowy cliff tiles, tall rock face topped with snow for snowy highlands: 1) snowy cliff face 2) snowy cliff corner 3) icy rock face 4) snow ledge 5) frosty drop 6) snow-capped stone wall

isometric castle stone wall tiles, tall grey battlement wall segments: 1) castle wall segment 2) wall with crenellations 3) wall corner tower base 4) arched gate wall 5) buttressed wall 6) mossy castle wall
isometric town wall and fence tiles, tall boundary walls: 1) stone town wall 2) wooden palisade 3) brick wall 4) wall with wooden gate 5) low garden wall 6) stone wall corner
isometric stone stairs and ramp tiles to connect elevation levels: 1) stone staircase up 2) stone ramp 3) grassy earthen ramp 4) wide steps 5) stair corner 6) short step ledge
isometric tall mountain rock tiles for high peaks: 1) tall grey mountain block 2) snow-capped peak 3) craggy summit 4) steep rock face 5) dark basalt spire 6) layered mountain strata

procedural._comment: When the explicit list is exhausted, keep inventing focused categories to grow the library forever.

categories.description: isometric flower meadow tiles, all flowering grass only: 1) red poppies in grass 2) blue flowers 3) purple lavender 4) mixed wildflowers 5) daisy field 6) yellow buttercups
isometric jungle floor tiles, all jungle ground only: 1) dense green undergrowth 2) muddy jungle path 3) mossy jungle rock 4) fern-covered ground 5) vines on soil 6) dark rainforest floor
isometric tundra tiles, all cold tundra only: 1) frosty grass 2) lichen-covered rock 3) patchy snow on grass 4) frozen mud 5) hardy moss 6) icy gravel
isometric gravel/path tiles, all loose stone only: 1) grey gravel 2) fine crushed stone 3) mixed pebbles 4) dark gravel 5) sandy gravel 6) rocky path
isometric desert mesa cliff tiles, tall red-rock canyon walls: 1) red mesa cliff face 2) sandstone layers 3) desert cliff corner 4) eroded rock wall 5) dusty ledge 6) canyon drop
